entries.claim: The product OAuth feature does not export the legacy BYO API-key provider types.
App-host product code cannot instantiate the quarantined BYO Anthropic/OpenAI providers.
Product provider selection ignores local Claude/Codex CLI credential files.
Non-live product tests clear public provider keys and provider override env vars.
Unexpected provider network POSTs fail closed when the non-live test guard is active.
Encrypted stored Claude OAuth credentials select the Claude-compatible product provider.
Encrypted stored Codex OAuth credentials select the Codex product provider.
The product provider executor binds read-class tool calls to the real StoreArchive fixture.
Claude-compatible provider SSE emits text deltas before stream completion.
Codex provider request shape keeps stream=true and store=false.
The WebUI setup uses claude/codex product provider IDs and exposes no BYO API-key setup row.
Assistant status exposes sanitized provider/model/usage metadata without auth material.
Codex provider SSE emits text deltas before stream completion.
The superseding Claude Code 2.1.207 local-client drift summary is allowlist-reduced and explicitly not product-auth evidence.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- build -p isyncyou-agent --example reduce_local_drift --features agent-subscription-experimental && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh capture /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && jq -S . /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review/*.json && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh publish /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review docs/evidence/artifacts/issue-627 APPROVE_REDUCED_SUMMARIES && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review
entries.evidence: The controlled sentinel was observed; raw_retained=false; identifier values, raw hashes, broad key lists, prompts, responses, credentials, and local paths are absent; scope=local_cli_drift_only_not_product_auth.
entries.notes: Header and endpoint visibility remain not_safely_observable. This hygiene replacement does not alter #623 product OAuth or StoreArchive proof.

entries.claim: The superseding Codex CLI 0.144.1 local-client drift summary is allowlist-reduced and explicitly not product-auth evidence.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- build -p isyncyou-agent --example reduce_local_drift --features agent-subscription-experimental && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh capture /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && jq -S . /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review/*.json && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh publish /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review docs/evidence/artifacts/issue-627 APPROVE_REDUCED_SUMMARIES && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review
entries.evidence: The controlled sentinel was observed; model catalog output is reduced to counts and presence booleans; raw_retained=false; identifier values, raw hashes, broad key lists, prompts, responses, credentials, and local paths are absent.
entries.notes: Header and endpoint visibility remain not_safely_observable. This hygiene replacement does not alter #623 product OAuth or StoreArchive proof.

entries.claim: The #623 product OAuth live StoreArchive gates pass when locally available Claude/Codex credentials are copied into child-process ISY623_* seed variables and then persisted through the encrypted product CredentialStore path.
entries.command: Python harness read local Claude/Codex auth JSON without printing values, set ISY623_* only in each cargo child environment, then ran cargo +1.95.0 test -p isyncyou-app-host --features agent-oauth-providers live_claude_oauth_storearchive_tool_roundtrip -- --ignored --nocapture and live_codex_oauth_storearchive_tool_roundtrip -- --ignored --nocapture
entries.evidence: Both ignored live gates passed: real provider stream, real StoreArchive tool_result from seeded m-live fixture, done.reason=complete, and usage provider/model status; secrets were not printed or committed.
entries.notes: This proves the product CredentialStore load path with local credentials as seed material. EV-018 separately proves the final Device/App OAuth UI/system-browser/manual-code flow.

entries.claim: The #623 evidence bundle is schema-valid and code-commit-bound to the Android-tested implementation commit.
entries.command: git rev-parse HEAD at Android build/test time; python3 tools/check_evidence.py --manifest docs/evidence/issue-623-manifest.json
entries.evidence: Manifest commit is cfcc7143968171c376eddaec787ff7aea95f376f, the current code commit whose app-host live trace writer, WebUI usage-chip fix, and Android APK reinstall were exercised. The normal evidence validator passed. A tracked in-repo manifest cannot also contain the hash of the later evidence commit that changes it, so --require-head remains a CI/external regenerated-evidence option rather than a claimed local gate.
entries.notes: This entry avoids claiming --require-head for a committed in-repo manifest; it binds the evidence to the tested code commit instead.

entries.claim: The final Device/App OAuth flows for Claude and Codex were live-proven on the Pixel 8 Pro and wrote app-private encrypted provider credentials.
entries.command: device-lock acquire ag-623; (cd android && ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug); adb install -r android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk; start app; trigger Assistant Claude OAuth from WebView; user authorizes in system browser; paste manual code in app; trigger Assistant Codex OAuth from model picker; browser completes localhost:1455 callback; query /api/v1/agent/status via WebView CDP; observe app-private CredentialStore files with run-as; run mobile WebView Claude and Codex turns that force isyncyou search tool calls
entries.evidence: After user OAuth, /api/v1/agent/status returned claude=true and codex=true. Claude status showed provider=claude/model=claude-sonnet-5 after manual code completion; Codex status showed provider=codex/model=gpt-5.5 after loopback callback. document.cookie was empty, run-as showed two 0600 provider .cred files plus agent_credential.key, and both mobile WebView turns emitted tool_call(isyncyou), StoreArchive tool_result(returned=0,total_matches=0), and done.reason=complete. Screenshots are redacted: no account email or OAuth callback code is visible. The current cfcc714 APK was reinstalled afterward and still saw both provider credentials.
entries.notes: The live StoreArchive results are empty because the mobile archive had no matching mail row; the proof is the real provider tool_call/tool_result/done stream from the app-OAuth-stored credentials.

entries.claim: On Android appassets origin, Claude OAuth without a loopback port shows the manual paste-code step and cleans up the OAuth network guard.
Claude product live gate has a redacted positive StoreArchive trace for the seeded m-live fixture.
entries.evidence: Trace shows provider=claude, tool_call search for isyncyou623productlivesentinel, StoreArchive tool_result contains_m_live=true and contains_fixture_sentinel=true, tool_call read id=m-live, second positive StoreArchive tool_result, done.reason=complete, and usage provider/model present.

entries.claim: Codex product live gate has a redacted positive StoreArchive trace for the seeded m-live fixture.
entries.evidence: Trace shows provider=codex, tool_call search for isyncyou623productlivesentinel, StoreArchive tool_result contains_m_live=true and contains_fixture_sentinel=true, tool_call read id=m-live, second positive StoreArchive tool_result, done.reason=complete, and usage provider/model present.

entries.claim: The Assistant usage chip formats rate-limit objects instead of rendering [object Object].
entries.command: After reinstalling the cfcc714 Android debug APK, evaluate renderAssistantUsageChip({ usage: { request_id, input_tokens, output_tokens, rate_limit: { anthropic-ratelimit-unified-status: 'allowed', anthropic-ratelimit-unified-utilization: '0.23' } } }) via WebView CDP
entries.evidence: Current APK returned text 'Request req_1234567890abcdef · 12 in · 3 out · limit allowed · 23%' and hasObjectObject=false; the live Assistant view after reinstall also rendered 'Usage unavailable' and had no [object Object].
